criteria.name: Problem Definition
criteria.description: Clearly defines the indexing and query optimization problem based on given data.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The problem is articulated with specific terms related to indexing and query performance, outlining key objectives.
criteria.poor_indicator: The problem is vaguely defined with little to no reference to indexing or query optimization concepts.

criteria.name: Contextual Relevance
criteria.description: Identifies relevant background information and context that influences the indexing strategy.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Context is well-researched, referencing specific database schemas or data characteristics that impact indexing.
criteria.poor_indicator: Context is missing or irrelevant, failing to connect to the indexing and query optimization challenge.

criteria.name: Task Specification
criteria.description: Translates the ill-defined problem into a well-defined task with clear objectives.
criteria.excellent_indicator: The task includes measurable goals, specifying desired outcomes for indexing and query performance.
criteria.poor_indicator: The task lacks clarity and measurable objectives, making it difficult to assess progress.

typical_errors: Confusing indexing with other database concepts like normalization.
Neglecting to consider the specific database management system in use.
Failing to outline the performance metrics relevant to the problem.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Effectively identifies errors or gaps in the AI-generated output related to indexing.
criteria.excellent_indicator: All significant errors are identified, with clear explanations of their implications on query performance.
criteria.poor_indicator: Few or no errors are identified, or the identified errors are trivial and do not impact the overall indexing strategy.

criteria.name: Relevance of Feedback
criteria.description: Assesses the relevance of the AI output to the original problem and task.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Feedback clearly aligns with the outlined problem and task, highlighting critical issues.
criteria.poor_indicator: Feedback is unrelated to the problem or lacks focus on indexing and optimization aspects.

criteria.name: Depth of Analysis
criteria.description: Provides a deep analysis of the AI output, considering various indexing strategies.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Analysis includes multiple perspectives on indexing and optimization, considering trade-offs and implications.
criteria.poor_indicator: Analysis is superficial, lacking depth or consideration of different indexing strategies.

typical_errors: Overlooking minor errors that can significantly affect performance.
Focusing only on syntactical errors instead of logical issues.
Failing to connect feedback to specific indexing strategies.

criteria.name: Corrective Commands
criteria.description: Provides clear and precise commands to correct AI outputs.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Commands are specific, actionable, and directly address identified errors in indexing.
criteria.poor_indicator: Commands are vague or do not directly address the issues identified in the AI output.

criteria.name: Iterative Improvement
criteria.description: Demonstrates an iterative approach to refining the AI output based on feedback.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Shows a clear progression in improvements with each iteration, enhancing indexing effectiveness.
criteria.poor_indicator: No noticeable improvement across iterations, or the same errors are repeated without adjustments.

criteria.name: Conceptual Clarity
criteria.description: Exhibits a strong understanding of indexing and optimization principles in steering commands.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Commands reflect a deep understanding of database concepts, leading to effective steering.
criteria.poor_indicator: Commands show a lack of understanding of indexing principles, resulting in ineffective guidance.

typical_errors: Issuing commands that contradict established indexing practices.
Not considering the database size or structure when steering.
Failing to provide sufficient detail in commands for effective changes.
